name: Human Body
description: Anatomy, physiology, and how we work

questions.q: Approximately how many bones does a human baby have at birth?

source.notes: Many fuse together during development. Adults have ~206.

questions.q: What is the largest organ in the human body?

source.notes: Average adult skin weighs ~8 lbs and covers ~20 sq ft.

questions.q: Which chemical is released by the body during allergic reactions, causing sneezing and itching?

source.notes: Antihistamines (like Benadryl) block its effects.

questions.q: What small flap of tissue prevents food from entering the windpipe during swallowing?

source.notes: Closes over the trachea when you swallow.

questions.q: Which organ is famous for having a minimal/unknown primary function and often becomes inflamed?

source.notes: Appendicitis affects ~7% of people. Recent research suggests it may help immunity.

questions.q: The meninges are protective membranes that cover which organs?

source.notes: Three layers: dura mater, arachnoid mater, pia mater. Meningitis = inflammation.

questions.q: Which tiny gland in the brain produces melatonin and regulates sleep?

source.notes: Descartes called it the 'seat of the soul.' Pea-sized, located near the brain's center.

questions.q: The adrenal glands sit on top of which organs?

source.notes: Also called 'suprarenal glands.' Produce adrenaline, cortisol, and other hormones.

questions.q: What is the main function of the gallbladder?

source.notes: Stores bile from the liver. Releases it into the small intestine to aid fat digestion.

questions.q: Tiny finger-like projections in the small intestine that absorb nutrients are called what?

source.notes: Each villus is covered with microvilli, enormously increasing surface area for absorption.

questions.q: How many chambers does the human heart have?

source.notes: Two atria (upper) and two ventricles (lower).

questions.q: What is the hardest substance in the human body?

source.notes: Made of hydroxyapatite. Harder than bone, which contains collagen.

questions.q: Which organ is the body's primary detoxification center, processing over 500 functions?

source.notes: Can regenerate itself after significant damage. Weighs ~3 lbs.

questions.q: How many pairs of chromosomes does a normal human cell contain?

source.notes: 23 pairs = 46 total. The 23rd pair determines sex (XX or XY).

questions.q: The aorta is the body's largest what?

source.notes: Carries oxygenated blood from the heart's left ventricle to the rest of the body.

questions.q: Approximately how many sweat glands does the average human have?

source.notes: Most concentrated on the palms, soles, forehead, and armpits. Two types: eccrine and apocrine.

questions.q: Keratin, a tough protein, is the main structural component of what?

source.notes: Also found in animal horns, hooves, and feathers. Different types for different tissues.

questions.q: Which bone group contains the sacrum, ilium, and pubis?

source.notes: Together form the hip bones. Differ slightly between male and female.

questions.q: What are the Y-shaped proteins produced by the immune system to fight pathogens called?

source.notes: Also called immunoglobulins. Made by B lymphocytes. Attach to antigens.

questions.q: Approximately what percentage of an adult human's body weight is made up of water?

source.notes: ~55-60% for adults. Infants are higher (~78%). Varies with body fat.
